decks.notes: This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly developing a board presence with efficient creatures like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Archon of Emeria. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression and disruption, utilizing cards like Collected Company to generate value and maintain pressure. Skyclave Apparition and Badgermole Cub provide versatile removal options, while Boseiju, Who Endures offers protection against counterspells. The deck's strategy revolves around efficient creatures and disruptive elements to secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing low-cost aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, to quickly deal damage to the opponent. Questing Druid provides ramp, while Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Cori-Steel Cutter offer additional ways to push through damage. The deck aims to establish early pressure and maintain a fast pace of play, overwhelming the opponent before they can stabilize. The inclusion of Den of the Bugbear and other lands that can produce red mana ensures consistency in casting the deck's spells.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient creatures like Llanowar Elves and Badgermole Cub. The deck uses disruptive cards like Archon of Emeria and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to slow down opponents, while also generating card advantage with cards like Collected Company and Skyclave Apparition. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with its aggressive creatures and disrupt their game plan with its disruptive elements, ultimately winning through combat damage.

The deck focuses on aggressive play with a mix of creatures and spells that deal direct damage to the opponent. It aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total through a combination of efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage. The deck also includes cards like Academic Dispute and Stormchaser's Talent for card advantage and tempo plays. Overall, the deck seeks to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and relentless assault while maintaining a proactive game plan.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to quickly deploy aggressive creatures such as Giada, Font of Hope, Resplendent Angel, and Skyclave Apparition onto the battlefield, while also utilizing cards like Collected Company and Llanowar Elves to ramp up mana and maintain board presence. The deck also includes cards like Righteous Valkyrie and Sigarda, Font of Blessings to boost the power of your creatures and gain incremental advantages over your opponent. The inclusion of efficient removal spells like Michelangelo's Technique helps to disrupt your opponent's game plan and secure victories through combat damage.

This deck focuses on utilizing efficient removal and disruption spells while also generating value through its creatures. It seeks to control the game by using cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Abrupt Decay to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse provide additional threats and ways to generate advantage. The deck also includes a mix of lands to ensure consistency and flexibility in casting its spells. Overall, the deck aims to outlast and outvalue the opponent in a grindy game.

This deck focuses on quickly playing low-cost creatures and spells in order to deal damage to the opponent as fast as possible. By combining burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage with creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and force the opponent to react defensively. Utilizing cards like Academic Dispute for card advantage and Stormchaser's Talent for additional damage, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and win the game before they can stabilize.

This UR Aggro deck relies on fast, low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger to quickly overwhelm opponents. Spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage provide additional removal and damage, while cards like Riverpyre Verge and Boomerang Basics allow for some control over the board. The deck aims to maintain strong board presence and apply pressure early on to secure a swift victory against opponents. The inclusion of cards like Soul-Scar Mage and Stormchaser's Talent further enhances the deck's ability to deal damage efficiently and effectively.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to create a fast-paced and aggressive gameplay, utilizing low-cost and efficient spells and creatures to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck focuses on a combination of burn spells, efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, and card draw through spells like Academic Dispute and Stock Up to maintain pressure on the opponent. The mana base is optimized for quick access to both red and blue mana sources, allowing for flexibility in casting spells. Overall, the deck aims to swarm the board and overwhelm the opponent with speed and efficiency.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on a fast-paced, aggressive playstyle with low-cost creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to control the board and remove threats. The inclusion of cards like Academic Dispute and Experimental Synthesizer allows for card advantage and flexibility in combat. The mana base is built around efficient lands like Spirebluff Canal and Steam Vents to ensure consistency in casting spells. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through card advantage, counterspells, removal, and efficient threats. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating incremental advantages over time. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion provides additional value and synergy with the deck's creature base. The mana base is carefully crafted to support the deck's game plan, ensuring consistent access to the necessary colors and mana sources. The deck excels in longer games where it can outvalue the opponent and eventually win through incremental advantage.

The main concept of this deck is to use a combination of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck also includes support cards like Iron-Shield Elf and Guardian of New Benalia to protect and enhance the combo pieces. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push are included to control the opponent's resources, while Monument to Endurance and Lively Dirge provide additional support for the strategy. The deck is designed to be efficient and fast-paced, aiming to win the game quickly through the combo synergy.

The deck focuses on controlling the opponent's plays through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It aims to disrupt the opponent's strategies while gradually building up its own resources. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion provides additional support for the control strategy. The deck also has a strong mana base to ensure consistent access to its spells. Overall, the key concept is to outlast the opponent and gradually gain an advantage through careful decision-making and resource management.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying creatures and disrupting opponents through cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Skyclave Apparition. It aims to overwhelm opponents with aggressive creatures like Archon of Emeria and Aven Interrupter, while also utilizing mana acceleration from cards like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves. Collected Company provides additional creature summoning potential, while Elspeth, Storm Slayer acts as a powerful finisher. The deck also includes some land-based utility with Hushwood Verge and Multiversal Passage for ramp and fixing.

The deck focuses on utilizing the combo between Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful board state. It includes cards that can protect the combo pieces and disrupt opponent's strategies, such as Thoughtseize and Fatal Push. The mana base is designed to support the combo pieces while also providing flexibility in casting spells. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creatures and artifacts.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, using cards like Monument to Endurance and Parhelion II to create a powerful win condition. It includes cards that support the combo such as Fleeting Spirit and Guardian of New Benalia, while also including disruption like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board and protect the combo pieces. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful combination of creatures and spells.
